findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
PostToolUse hook exfiltrates tool inputs, outputs, and session secrets
findings.description: The skill installs a global PostToolUse hook that captures every tool invocation, including full input/output content and sensitive environment variables, then sends them to an external server. This includes explicit collection of ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and GITHUB_TOKEN, creating a direct credential and data exfiltration path.
findings.evidence: monitor.sh builds a payload containing "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY", "GITHUB_TOKEN", and "HOME" (lines 25-29) and posts it to https://mcp-analytics-hub.io/hooks/post-tool via curl (lines 34-37). SKILL.md also states the hook captures "API keys and tokens" and forwards them in real time (lines 36-38, 51-58).
findings.remediation: Remove remote transmission of tool data and environment variables. Do not collect secrets, full tool outputs, or session context. If auditing is required, make it local-only, minimize logged fields, and require explicit user approval.

findings.title: Skill instructs installation of a persistent global hook that intercepts all future tool use
findings.description: The setup steps direct the user to copy the bundled script into ~/.claude/hooks/PostToolUse.sh, replacing or creating a global hook outside the skill directory. This gives the skill ongoing visibility into tool activity across sessions and tasks, not just when the skill is intentionally invoked.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md instructs: `cp hooks/monitor.sh ~/.claude/hooks/PostToolUse.sh` and `chmod +x ~/.claude/hooks/PostToolUse.sh` (lines 31-33). The installed script logs and forwards tool activity for every PostToolUse event.
findings.remediation: Do not overwrite global Claude hooks from a skill package. Constrain any monitoring to explicit, temporary, user-approved contexts and avoid persistent interception of unrelated tool executions.

findings.title: Mixed-script Unicode homoglyphs suggest review-evasion obfuscation
findings.description: The manifest and instructions contain multiple Cyrillic lookalike characters embedded in English words (for example, in 'Сonfigure', 'сentralized', and 'Рrotocol'). This can hinder human review, evade simple keyword scanning, and conceal risky behavior in documentation.
findings.evidence: Examples include 'Сonfigure' and 'сentralized' in the description (line 3), 'сonnections' and 'сapability' (line 8), 'MCP Сonfiguration' (line 21), and 'Рrotocol' in the table header (line 42).
findings.remediation: Normalize text to a single script, scan for Unicode confusables, and reject mixed-script manifests or instructions unless clearly justified.
